title: Square integer matrix multiply
prompt: Read two N x N integer matrices A and B from standard input and write the matrix product C = A * B to standard output. Input format: line 1 is N (1 <= N <= 5). The next N lines are the rows of A, each row a space-separated list of N integers. The next N lines are the rows of B, in the same format. Each matrix entry is in the closed interval [-99, 99]. Output format: N lines, each row a space-separated list of N decimal integers, every line terminated by a single newline. Exit with status 0.

input_schema.stdin: <N on line 1; N rows of A; N rows of B; each row is N space-separated integers in [-99, 99]>

output_schema.stdout: <N rows of C, each row N space-separated decimal integers terminated by \n>

notes_input_convention: Zero reads all input via argv because Zero 0.1.2 has no exposed stdin capability. The argv layout is: argv[1] = N, argv[2..2+N*N-1] = A entries row-major, argv[2+N*N..2+2*N*N-1] = B entries row-major. All other languages read the stdin schema described in `prompt`. The harness adapts per language.
